description: Reduce 19 WASM modules to 7 focused on multi-host dispatch. Migrate inference to procedures for direct LLM calls from SpacetimeDB. Wire remote agent state to SpacetimeDB for real-time fleet visibility across hosts and Docker sandboxes.
blocks: Remote Docker sandbox dispatch (agents can't coordinate without SpacetimeDB-backed fleet state)
Multi-host swarm execution (dashboard can't see remote agents without subscription-based registry)
successCriteria: Only 7 WASM modules in spacetime-modules/ (5 kept + 2 absorbed into hexflo-coordination)
STDB_MODULE_DATABASES in hex-core/src/lib.rs lists exactly 7 entries
Docker sandbox agent on remote host claims task via SpacetimeDB subscription (no hex-nexus polling)
Dashboard shows remote agent fleet in real-time via WebSocket subscription
Inference request from sandboxed agent completes via procedure (no hex-nexus proxy)
cargo build -p hex-nexus compiles clean with zero dead-module references
hex nexus start + hex swarm init + hex task create works end-to-end

riskRegister.risk: SpacetimeDB procedures are newer; may have undiscovered edge cases in HTTP calls
riskRegister.mitigation: P2 keeps hex-nexus direct inference path as fallback; procedure path opt-in via feature flag

riskRegister.risk: Deleting 12 modules may break spacetime_bindings imports that are referenced but dead
riskRegister.mitigation: P1 includes cargo build gate after each module deletion batch

riskRegister.risk: Active swarms may reference deleted module databases during migration
riskRegister.mitigation: P0 absorbs fleet-state and hexflo-lifecycle tables before any deletion; P1 checks for active swarms

riskRegister.risk: RemoteRegistryAdapter replacement (P4) may break SSH tunnel agent flow
riskRegister.mitigation: P4 maintains in-memory cache backed by SpacetimeDB subscription; fallback to cache on STDB timeout

phases.name: Absorb fleet-state and hexflo-lifecycle into hexflo-coordination
phases.description: Move compute_node table from fleet-state and lifecycle event tables from hexflo-lifecycle into hexflo-coordination module. This preserves data while reducing module count.

tasks.name: Add compute_node table + reducers to hexflo-coordination/src/lib.rs
tasks.description: Copy ComputeNode struct, register_node, update_health, increment_agents, decrement_agents reducers from fleet-state/src/lib.rs into hexflo-coordination. Preserve exact field names and types for backward compatibility.

tasks.name: Merge hexflo-lifecycle phase transition reducers into hexflo-coordination
tasks.description: Move swarm phase transition tables and reducers from hexflo-lifecycle into hexflo-coordination. These are tightly coupled to swarm state and should not be a separate module.

tasks.name: Merge hexflo-cleanup reducers into hexflo-coordination
tasks.description: Move stale agent detection and task reclamation logic from hexflo-cleanup into hexflo-coordination. Deduplicate with existing agent_mark_stale/agent_mark_dead reducers.

tasks.name: Verify hexflo-coordination compiles and publishes with merged tables
tasks.description: Build the WASM module, publish to local SpacetimeDB, verify all tables exist via spacetime sql.

phases.name: Delete 12 dead WASM modules and prune bindings
phases.description: Remove modules that are deployed but never invoked. Update STDB_MODULE_DATABASES, spacetime_bindings, spacetime_launcher, and spacetime_state.rs references.

tasks.name: Delete 12 module directories from spacetime-modules/
tasks.description: Remove: file-lock-manager, inference-bridge, architecture-enforcer, skill-registry, hook-registry, agent-definition-registry, workplan-state, chat-relay, hexflo-lifecycle (absorbed in P0), hexflo-cleanup (absorbed in P0), conflict-resolver, test-results. Keep fleet-state directory until P0 verified, then delete.

tasks.name: Update STDB_MODULE_DATABASES in hex-core/src/lib.rs
tasks.description: Reduce constant to 7 entries: hexflo-coordination (hex), agent-registry, inference-gateway, secret-grant, rl-engine, fleet-state (now in hex db via hexflo-coordination — remove entry or map to hex). Update tier structure accordingly. Remove neural-lab entry.

tasks.name: Prune spacetime_bindings/ — remove bindings for deleted modules
tasks.description: Delete auto-generated binding directories for removed modules in hex-nexus/src/spacetime_bindings/. Should reduce from 118 files (~20,353 LOC) to ~40 files. Update spacetime_bindings/mod.rs to only export remaining modules.

tasks.name: Update spacetime_launcher/ publication pipeline
tasks.description: Reduce tiered publication from 5 tiers to 2. Tier 0: hexflo-coordination, agent-registry. Tier 1: inference-gateway, secret-grant, rl-engine. Remove references to deleted modules.

tasks.name: Remove dead module calls from spacetime_state.rs
tasks.description: Remove methods in spacetime_state.rs that call reducers on deleted modules. Expect to reduce from 1,775 lines to ~800 lines. Remove cross-database calls (call_reducer_on, query_table_on) for deleted modules.

tasks.name: Update Cargo workspace — remove deleted module members
tasks.description: Remove deleted modules from root Cargo.toml workspace members list. Verify cargo build -p hex-nexus -p hex-cli -p hex-agent all compile clean.

phases.name: Migrate inference-gateway to procedure-based LLM calls
phases.description: Leverage SpacetimeDB procedures to make direct HTTP calls to LLM providers from inside the WASM module. Sandboxed agents write to inference_request table; procedure calls API; result appears in inference_response table via subscription.

tasks.name: Implement execute_inference procedure with HTTP client
tasks.description: Expand existing execute_inference procedure in inference-gateway to make real HTTP POST to LLM provider URLs. Read provider config from inference_provider table. Read API keys from inference_api_key table. Support Anthropic Messages API and OpenAI-compatible format. Write response to inference_response table via complete_inference reducer.

tasks.name: Add request_inference reducer for agent-initiated inference
tasks.description: Reducer that writes to inference_request table with: request_id, agent_id, model, messages_json, max_tokens, temperature. Triggers execute_inference procedure via scheduled callback or table subscription.

tasks.name: Add SpacetimeDB subscription path in hex-agent daemon mode
tasks.description: In hex-agent daemon mode, subscribe to inference_response table filtered by agent_id. When a response appears, parse it and feed into the conversation loop. This is the path sandboxed agents use — they write inference_request, subscribe to inference_response.

tasks.name: Keep hex-nexus direct inference path as fallback
tasks.description: hex-nexus retains the ability to proxy inference directly for local agents (lower latency). Add feature flag stdb-inference-procedure (default on) to toggle between procedure path and direct proxy path. Local agents use direct; sandboxed/remote agents use procedure.

tasks.name: Integration test: sandboxed agent inference via procedure
tasks.description: Test: spawn Docker sandbox -> hex-agent daemon writes inference_request -> procedure calls mock LLM endpoint -> response appears in inference_response -> agent receives via subscription. Verify end-to-end latency and error handling.

phases.name: Migrate agent cleanup to scheduled procedure
phases.description: Move stale/dead agent detection from hex-nexus background task to a scheduled SpacetimeDB procedure in agent-registry. Runs every 30s, marks agents stale (45s no heartbeat) or dead (120s), reclaims tasks from dead agents.

tasks.name: Add scheduled cleanup procedure to agent-registry module
tasks.description: Add #[spacetimedb::procedure] that queries AgentHeartbeat table, compares last_seen with current time, marks agents stale (>45s) or dead (>120s). For dead agents, query hexflo-coordination for assigned tasks and set them back to pending. Schedule to run every 30 seconds.

tasks.name: Remove cleanup background task from hex-nexus
tasks.description: Remove the background tokio::spawn cleanup task in hex-nexus that polls agent heartbeats and marks stale/dead. This is now handled server-side by the procedure.

phases.name: Wire remote agent state to SpacetimeDB
phases.description: Replace in-memory RemoteRegistryAdapter (HashMap) with SpacetimeDB-backed state using hexflo-coordination tables. Dashboard subscribes for real-time fleet visibility. Agents on any host see the full fleet.

tasks.name: Add remote_agent table to hexflo-coordination module
tasks.description: Add RemoteAgent table with fields: agent_id (primary key), name, host, project_dir, status (connecting/online/busy/stale/dead), last_heartbeat, connected_at, tunnel_id, capabilities_json. Add reducers: register_remote_agent, update_remote_heartbeat, deregister_remote_agent.

tasks.name: Create SpacetimeDB-backed RemoteRegistryAdapter
tasks.description: New adapter implementing IRemoteRegistryPort that calls SpacetimeDB reducers instead of HashMap. Maintains local Arc<RwLock<HashMap>> cache backed by SpacetimeDB subscription for fast reads. On STDB timeout, falls back to cache. On register/deregister, writes to STDB first, then updates cache on confirmation.

tasks.name: Wire agent heartbeats to SpacetimeDB
tasks.description: Modify AgentLifecycleAdapter to write heartbeats to SpacetimeDB (update_remote_heartbeat reducer) instead of only updating in-memory registry. Agents on remote hosts are now visible to all subscribers.

tasks.name: Update dashboard to subscribe to remote_agent table
tasks.description: Replace HTTP polling of /api/remote-agents with SpacetimeDB WebSocket subscription to remote_agent table in hexflo-coordination. Agent cards update in real-time when agents connect, go stale, or die. Fleet overview aggregates from subscription, not REST.

tasks.name: Integration test: multi-host agent visibility
tasks.description: Test: register agent on simulated Host B via SSH spawn -> verify SpacetimeDB remote_agent table updated -> verify dashboard subscription receives agent -> simulate heartbeat timeout -> verify stale detection propagates to all subscribers.

phases.name: Remote Docker sandbox dispatch via fleet + SpacetimeDB
phases.description: Extend DockerSandboxAdapter to dispatch agents to remote Docker hosts selected via fleet-state (now in hexflo-coordination). Agents inside remote sandboxes coordinate entirely via SpacetimeDB subscriptions.

tasks.name: Extend DockerSandboxAdapter for remote Docker daemons
tasks.description: Add remote spawn path to DockerSandboxAdapter: SSH to selected fleet node -> docker sandbox create on remote host -> docker sandbox exec hex-agent daemon. Use FleetManager.select_node() for host selection. Pass SPACETIMEDB_HOST pointing to central SpacetimeDB instance (not host.docker.internal).

tasks.name: Update sandbox.yml for remote connectivity
tasks.description: Add configurable network allow-list entries for remote SpacetimeDB host (not just host.docker.internal). Support environment variable substitution in sandbox.yml for SPACETIMEDB_HOST, HEX_NEXUS_HOST.

tasks.name: Add hex agent spawn-remote --sandbox CLI command
tasks.description: Wire CLI subcommand: hex agent spawn-remote --host user@host --sandbox --project-dir /path. Calls POST /api/remote-agents/spawn-remote with sandbox=true flag, which triggers DockerSandboxAdapter remote path.

tasks.name: End-to-end test: remote Docker sandbox swarm task
tasks.description: Full flow: hex swarm init -> hex task create -> fleet selects remote host -> Docker sandbox created on remote -> hex-agent daemon starts inside sandbox -> claims task via SpacetimeDB subscription -> executes via MCP server tools -> writes result -> supervisor sees completion. Verify all coordination happens via SpacetimeDB, not hex-nexus HTTP.

phases.name: Finalize and verify
phases.description: Final verification, documentation update, and cleanup.

tasks.name: Update CLAUDE.md system components section
tasks.description: Update the SpacetimeDB section to reflect 7 modules (not 18). Update hex-nexus description to reflect focused role (filesystem bridge + dashboard + agent spawner, not coordination router). Update module list and tier structure.

tasks.name: Release build and smoke test full pipeline
tasks.description: cargo build --release for all workspace members. Run hex nexus start, hex swarm init, hex task create, hex agent spawn-remote --sandbox. Verify dashboard shows fleet, tasks complete, inference works through procedures.

mergeOrder: P1 (delete dead modules — depends on P0 completing absorption)
P2 + P3 parallel (inference procedures + cleanup procedures — independent of each other, both depend on P1)
P4 (remote agent state — depends on P0 for table structure)
P5 (remote Docker dispatch — depends on P2 inference + P4 fleet state)
done_condition: 7 WASM modules total. Docker sandbox agent on remote host completes swarm task end-to-end via SpacetimeDB coordination (no hex-nexus proxy for inference or task claiming). Dashboard shows real-time fleet via subscription.
